$comment: Hot-path registry. Each entry maps a hot-path source file to the performance contract tests that guard it. hot-path-coverage.test.js enforces this file: sources and contracts must exist, contracts must declare their sources in a `// @hot-paths:` header, and every source listed here must appear in the pipeline.ts `test.performance` task inputs so changes to a hot path automatically re-run this suite. Add an entry when code becomes a hot path; removing one is a conscious decision, not drift.

hotPaths.reason: Attribute prefix normalization backs every attribute read in activation scans; it must stay memoized by config identity.
DOM activation scans, boundary lookup, and swap/rescan run on every navigation and state-driven swap.
Every signal write, binding update, and boundary commit flows through flush/commit scheduling.
Signal reads and writes are per-binding operations; write coalescing keeps DOM work constant per tick.
The transition planner decides the render/fetch/history work paid per navigation.
